mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns fall below 0.5% in 40%+ of triggered instances despite PAYEMS > 100k monthly gains.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signal weakening demand conditions that correlate with negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exceed 1.5% in 60%+ of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS decline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 1.5% signals strengthening supply-side dynamics that correlate with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 50%+ of triggered INDPRO growth events.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes that increase equity discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns exceed 1.0% in 50%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) decline below 4% signals tight labor markets that drive wage inflation and corporate margin pressures, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signal deteriorating demand conditions that correlate with negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns exceed 0.8% in 50%+ of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 2% combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong supply-side growth and labor market conditions that drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns remain negative in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO > 2% and UNRATE declines.
